name: Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM)
steps: Admit to inpatient care (TFC/SC) if: no appetite, medical complications, edema grade 3
Outpatient care (OTP) if: good appetite, no complications
warning: SAM with complications is a medical emergency — refer urgently.

name: Moderate Acute Malnutrition (MAM)
steps: Screen for and treat underlying infections
Discharge when MUAC ≥ 125mm for 2 consecutive visits
warning: Refer to SAM management if MUAC drops below 115mm or edema develops.

name: Uncomplicated Malaria
criteria: Positive RDT or blood smear for Plasmodium
steps: AL (Coartem): 6-dose regimen over 3 days with food
Advise to return if: not improving in 24h, vomiting, unable to take oral meds
warning: Any danger sign = severe malaria → parenteral artesunate + urgent referral.

name: Acute Watery Diarrhea / Cholera
criteria: No blood in stool (if blood → dysentery)
steps: Plan A (no dehydration): ORS 50–100ml/loose stool, zinc 10/20mg × 10 days
Plan B (some dehydration): ORS 75ml/kg over 4h in facility, then Plan A
Plan C (severe dehydration): Ringer's lactate 100ml/kg IV — refer urgently
Cholera suspected (cholera zone + severe dehydration): Ciprofloxacin 1g single dose (adult)
warning: Severe dehydration, shock, or altered consciousness → immediate IV fluids + referral.

name: Acute Respiratory Infection (ARI) / Pneumonia
steps: Non-severe pneumonia (fast breathing, no chest indrawing): Amoxicillin 25mg/kg twice daily × 5 days
Severe pneumonia (chest indrawing): Amoxicillin high-dose 40mg/kg twice daily + refer
Very severe / danger signs: Ampicillin + gentamicin IV + urgent referral
No antibiotics for viral URTI (cough < 2 weeks, no fast breathing)
warning: Any danger sign → very severe disease → urgent referral + IV antibiotics.

name: Dysentery (Bloody Diarrhea)
criteria: May have fever, abdominal cramps, tenesmus
steps: Treat for Shigella: Ciprofloxacin 500mg twice daily × 3 days (adult); Ceftriaxone 50mg/kg once daily × 5 days (child)
Treat for amoebic dysentery if not responding: Metronidazole 30–50mg/kg/day in 3 doses × 5 days
warning: Severe dehydration or signs of HUS (pallor, oliguria) → urgent referral.
